title: Distinguish :inline vs :manual Oban testing modes
prompt: Write `docs/oban_testing_modes.md` comparing Oban's `:inline` and `:manual` testing modes. Must include: (1) `:inline` executes jobs synchronously in the calling process with no DB for job bookkeeping, (2) `:manual` inserts jobs into the DB but does not execute them — they can be asserted via `assert_enqueued`, (3) `:inline` does not work for jobs that depend on the sandbox transaction (the worker would see the test's rolled-back data), (4) `:manual` does work because no execution actually happens.
tier_rationale: Reference doc — requires knowing both modes and their tradeoffs.
research_citation: "`:inline`—jobs execute immediately within the calling process and without touching the database." — Oban testing docs
